pattern: Merging always folds a smaller-or-equal left neighbor into the one on its right, so scanning from the right and accumulating a running total captures every merge that will ever happen; a strictly larger element you meet breaks the chain and starts a fresh running total.
hints: A merge only ever combines an element into its right neighbor, and only when it is no bigger than that neighbor -- so think about building runs from the right end of the array.
Scan from the last element toward the first, keeping a running total that represents 'everything merged into this point so far'.

If the next element to the left is less than or equal to the running total, fold it in (add it to the running total); otherwise the chain breaks here -- record the running total as a candidate answer and start a new running total at this element. The final answer is the largest running total seen, including the very last one.

edge_inputs.description: all equal values merge completely into one sum
strictly decreasing values, no merge is ever possible
strictly increasing values merge all the way through
two maximum-value elements merge into double
a large value near the front breaks the merge chain from the right
two separate breaks in the merge chain
a run of small values merges completely into a trailing large one

stress.description: large random array of positive values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
